methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Tritone Technologies is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ofer Ben-Zur. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of six same-stage, same-sector peers, supplemented by one public comparable (ticker: 277810.KQ) and eight additional private benchmarks including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

The peer-median method, the sole quantitative approach applicable at this stage, yields $1.1B. This reflects the median valuation across the six-company peer set, which ranges from $300M (Fairmat) to $10.0B (1X Technologies). The wide dispersion—a four-magnitude spread between floor and ceiling—reflects material heterogeneity within Series B robotics financing, driven by differences in technical approach, deployment readiness, and investor backing.

The triangulated valuation supports a range of $914M to $1.2B, with a median of $1.1B. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given the sector's nascency and varying definitions of "robotics"; the absence of revenue or growth-rate data to weight comparables; and Tritone's undisclosed prior fundraising, which constrains assessment of capital efficiency. The valuation is defensible as a market median but carries material estimation risk absent additional methods or disclosure of Tritone's financial performance.
